units.unit_name: Project in Economics Guidelines as given in Class XII curriculum

units.unit_name: Development Experience (1947-90) and Economic Reforms since 1991

units.unit_name: Development Experience of India: A comparison with neighbours

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.chapter_name: What is Economics? Meaning, scope, functions and importance of statistics in Economics

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.unit_name: Collection, Organisation and Presentation of Data
chapters.chapter_name: Census of India and National Sample Survey Organisation.

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.unit_name: Collection, Organisation and Presentation of Data
chapters.chapter_name: Meaning and types of variables; Frequency Distribution.

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.unit_name: Collection, Organisation and Presentation of Data
chapters.chapter_name: Tabular Presentation and Diagrammatic Presentation of Data: (i) Geometric forms (bar diagrams and pie diagrams), (ii) Frequency diagrams (histogram, polygon and Ogive) and (iii) Arithmetic line graphs (time series graph).

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.chapter_name: Measures of Central Tendency - Arithmetic mean, Median and Mode Correlation – meaning and properties, scatter diagram; measures of correlation - Karl Pearson's method (two variables ungrouped data) Spearman's rank correlation (Non-Repeated Ranks and Repeated Ranks). Introduction to Index Numbers - meaning, types - Wholesale Price Index, Consumer Price Index and index of industrial production, uses of index numbers; Inflation and Index Numbers, Simple Aggregative Method.

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.chapter_name: what, how and for whom to produce; concepts of Production Possibility Frontier and Opportunity Cost.

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.chapter_name: Consumer's equilibrium - meaning of Utility, Marginal Utility, Law of Diminishing Marginal Utility, conditions of consumer's equilibrium using marginal utility analysis. Indifference curve analysis of consumer's equilibrium-the consumer's budget (budget set and budget line), preferences of the consumer (indifference curve, indifference map) and conditions of consumer's equilibrium.

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.chapter_name: Demand, market demand, determinants of demand, demand schedule, demand curve and its slope, movement along and shifts in the demand curve; price elasticity of demand - factors affecting price elasticity of demand; measurement of price elasticity of demand – percentage-change method and total expenditure method.

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.chapter_name: Meaning of Production Function – Short-Run and Long-Run Total Product, Average Product and Marginal Product. Returns to a Factor Cost – Short run costs - Total Cost, Total Fixed Cost, Total Variable Cost; Average Cost; Average Fixed Cost, Average Variable Cost and Marginal Cost - meaning and their relationships.

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.chapter_name: Revenue – Total Revenue, Average Revenue and Marginal Revenue - meaning and their relationship. Producer's Equilibrium - meaning and its conditions in terms of Marginal Revenue- Marginal Cost.

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.chapter_name: Supply, market supply, determinants of supply, supply schedule, supply curve and its slope, movements along and shifts in supply curve, price elasticity of supply; measurement of price elasticity of supply - percentage-change method.

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.unit_name: Perfect Competition - Price Determination and simple applications

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.chapter_name: consumption goods, capital goods, final goods, intermediate goods; stocks and flows; gross investment and depreciation. Circular flow of income (two sector model); Methods of calculating National Income - Value Added or Product method, Expenditure method, Income method.

chapters.subject: Economics
chapters.chapter_name: Gross National Product (GNP), Net National Product (NNP), Gross Domestic Product (GDP) and Net Domestic Product (NDP) - at market price, at factor cost; Real and Nominal GDP GDP Deflator, GDP and Welfare
